title: pandanus concretus subsp. circularis
intro: Pandanus concretus subsp. circularis is a subspecies of the pandanus plant, commonly known as a type of screwpine or pandanus palm, native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pandanus concretus subsp. circularis include the potential treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and infections, as well as aiding in the reduction of inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
